summary: TestCollector_Update_Verbose is a strong live-PG test that exercises the real Collector against actual /proc-backed stats with no mocks. All assertions pass the litmus test: each one fails if the corresponding implementation line is removed. It covers all three TDD anchors precisely — simultaneous Diskstats/Netdevs/Fsstats population, explicit verboseFirstTick set-then-clear (correctly avoiding the unreliable nil-slice signal), and OFF->ON re-arm without Reset() driven by prevVerboseActive. State-transition coverage is complete and ordered correctly. Matches the project connect-or-fail convention. Only minor (non-blocking) observations on edge coverage.

findings.issue: The == nil guard / side-panel-coexistence branch (verbose ON while a side panel already populated one source via collectExtra) is not exercised. The test always runs with collectExtra unset, so the guard's true branch (s.Diskstats already non-nil -> skip re-collect) never executes. AC line: '== nil guards prevent re-collecting a source already populated by the active side panel' has no direct assertion. This is a behavioral path with a real failure mode (double-collect would corrupt the prev/curr snapshot shift for that source).
findings.recommendation: Add a sub-case that sets c.config.collectExtra = CollectDiskstats AND view.Verbose = true, then asserts all three sources still populate (Netdevs/Fsstats via the verbose branch, Diskstats via the switch) in one Update. Optionally assert the disk snapshot was collected exactly once by checking the prev/curr disk snapshot is not double-shifted. This closes the guard coverage gap without adding a mock.

findings.issue: Assertions use assert.NotEqual(t, 0, len(...)) for the three slices. This proves non-empty but does not surface WHICH source failed in the failure message (a len==0 on Netdevs and on Fsstats produce identical-looking failures aside from the field name). Minor readability/diagnostics point, not a correctness issue.
findings.recommendation: Prefer assert.NotEmpty(t, stat.System.Netdevs, "netdevs must populate under verbose") with a per-source message, or assert.Greater(t, len(stat.System.Fsstats), 0, "fsstats ..."). Matches the style already used elsewhere in the file (assert.Greater with len). Purely diagnostic — does not change what is verified.

findings.issue: The OFF tick (Verbose=false) only asserts verboseFirstTick == false. It does not assert that prevVerboseActive was reset to false — the actual mechanism that arms the next OFF->ON re-enable. The subsequent re-enable assertion (line 197) indirectly proves it, so coverage is effectively complete, but the intermediate state transition is not asserted directly.
findings.recommendation: Optional: add assert.False(t, c.prevVerboseActive) right after the Verbose=false Update to document and pin the else-branch (c.prevVerboseActive = false) directly. Since the field is private and the test is same-package, this is accessible and would make the re-arm contract explicit rather than inferred.
